title: Delivery OS — architecture boundary config
description: The single declarative source of truth for the layer boundary (Repository Principle). The arch-boundary-guard and the standing Delete Test are driven entirely by this file; they carry no hard-coded folder names. Validated fail-closed: an invalid config refuses to run the gate.

layers.description: Layer membership. A file is classified by the LONGEST matching folder/file prefix; a layer's `exclude` re-pins a sub-path out of that layer (to whichever other layer claims it, else unclassified).

folders.description: Repo-relative folder prefixes or exact file paths (POSIX slashes) that belong to this layer.

exclude.description: Sub-paths re-pinned OUT of this layer (e.g. ports.ts -> contracts; adapters/ -> adapters; scripts/ -> unclassified test harness).

mayImport.description: The layers this layer is permitted to import. An edge to a layer NOT in this list is a [direction] violation. Dependencies must flow inward: adapter -> contract -> core.

denyInfraSdk.description: When true, a bare import of any infraSdkDenylist entry (and any infraCallDenylist inline pattern) is an [infra-sdk] violation. Core/Contracts = true; Adapters = false (SDKs legally live there).

exclude.description: Globally excluded from the scan: build output + vendored/consumer/demo trees + the throwaway delete-test worktree. Supports a trailing /** glob and bare directory names.

infraSdkDenylist.description: Infrastructure-plane SDKs forbidden in any denyInfraSdk layer. Matched by exact PACKAGE NAME (so `pg` matches `pg` and `pg/...` but NOT `drizzle-orm/pg-core`), or as a glob when the entry contains `*` (matched against the full specifier, e.g. `@slack/*`, `*runner*`).

re.description: A RegExp source tested per line in denyInfraSdk layers.

infraCallDenylist.description: Inline infrastructure-call patterns forbidden in denyInfraSdk layers (e.g. execFileSync of a relative-path tool = plane wiring). Carried over from the residency-guard.

coreTsconfig.description: The split tsconfig (core+contracts, adapters/scripts excluded). `tsc --noEmit -p <this>` is the 'Core builds' assertion.

toolchainNodeModules.description: A repo-relative node_modules carrying tsc + tsx + @types/node (the worktree has none of its own). The Delete Test runs the toolchain from HERE against the worktree sources.

coreSelfTests.description: REAL governance-engine self-tests (the tree under deletion, NOT a vendored copy). Run via tsx against the post-deletion worktree. They import the real organs, so a Core organ that illegitimately imported a deleted adapter would FAIL them.

subsystems.description: Per-subsystem delete matrix. A subsystem whose `remove` folders are all absent today is SKIPPED with a WARN (armed ahead of the code).

injectImportInto.description: A REAL Core organ to plant an illegitimate adapter import into (--prove-oracle mode).

injectImport.description: The illegitimate Core->adapter import line planted to prove the Delete Test oracle has teeth (it MUST then go red).
